summary: Completed testing of Iteration 16 features: Buzz Score Sentiment (-100 to +100) and Performance Formula. All 12 backend tests passed. Frontend features verified: (1) PlayingXIPerformance renders 22 buzz badges (11 per team) with green color and upward arrows for positive values, (2) ConsultantDashboard PlayerImpact shows buzz_score column with +0 for legacy cached data, (3) User Guide has 10 sections including updated Playing XI section with buzz score documentation, (4) Factor Breakdown shows two-sided Team1 vs Team2 layout, (5) Match State hidden for upcoming matches, (6) Simulation returns realistic probabilities (77.6% vs 22.4%, not 100/0).
passed_tests: Backend: apply_buzz_and_luck function correctly maps buzz_score to ±20% modifier (buzz_modifier within -0.25 to +0.25)
Backend: apply_buzz_and_luck applies luck factor ±15% (luck_factor within 0.80 to 1.20)
Backend: expected_runs values are reasonable and non-negative (0-200 range)
Backend: POST /api/matches/ipl2026_008/consult returns player_impact with buzz_score, buzz_reason, buzz_confidence fields
Backend: Player impact predicted_runs and predicted_wickets are non-negative
Backend: buzz_score defaults to 0 for legacy cached data (backward compat)
Backend: buzz_confidence backward compatibility maintained (0-100 range)
Backend: Simulation returns realistic probabilities (77.6% vs 22.4%, not 100/0)
Backend: Simulation has predicted scores (mean_team1_score: 152, mean_team2_score: 134)
Backend: edge_reasons array non-empty when market odds provided (3 reasons)
Backend: Playing XI players have buzz fields (buzz_score or buzz_confidence)
Backend: Playing XI players have expected_runs and expected_wickets
Backend: User Guide has 10 sections
Frontend: User Guide button (? icon) found in bottom-left
Frontend: User Guide modal opens with 10 sections
Frontend: User Guide Playing XI section mentions buzz score
Frontend: PlayingXIPerformance section found on pre-match page
Frontend: 22 buzz score badges rendered (11 per team)
Frontend: Buzz badges show positive values with green color and upward arrows
Frontend: Factor Breakdown shows two-sided layout (MI on left, DC on right)
Frontend: Match State section hidden for upcoming match (overs=0, score=0)
Frontend: Verdict section displays 'MI WINS' with 'STRONG' badge and 'STRONG VALUE' signal
Frontend: WHY THIS SIGNAL? section shows 3 edge reasons
Frontend: Simulation Summary shows predicted scores (152 vs 134)
Frontend: Player Impact section shows players with roles, predicted runs/wickets, and buzz_score (+0 for legacy)
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: consultant_engine.py player_impact was missing buzz_score, buzz_reason, buzz_confidence fields - added them to the player_impact dict
test_credentials: No authentication required - open access app
seed_data_creation: None - used existing match ipl2026_008 (MI vs DC) with cached Playing XI data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 16 features fully working. Key changes: (1) consultant_engine.py now includes buzz_score, buzz_reason, buzz_confidence in player_impact response. (2) Cached data for ipl2026_008 uses OLD format with buzz_confidence (0-100) but no buzz_score - frontend buzzDisplay() handles this with backward compat by treating old values as positive-only. (3) New fetches (which cost GPT API call) will use new format with buzz_score (-100 to +100) and buzz_reason. (4) Performance formula: expected = base_stats * (1 + buzz_modifier) * luck_factor where buzz_modifier = buzz_score/500 (maps -100 to +100 → -0.20 to +0.20) and luck_factor is random 0.85-1.15.

user_guide_sections.playing_xi_section: Updated with buzz score and performance formula docs

factor_breakdown_two_sided.layout: Team1 (MI) on left, Team2 (DC) on right

match_state_hidden.condition: Hidden when overs=0 and score=0 (upcoming matches)
